title: All Eligibility Rules Stored as Configuration — No Hardcoded Business Logic in Application Code
description: Eligibility rules shall be stored as configurable JSON in the data layer, not as if/else logic in application code. The rule engine evaluates rule records; it never contains hardcoded thresholds like 'if hours >= 30'. This is the core architectural principle enabling AI Requirements → Rule Config.
required: Rule engine reads eligibilityRules.json to get threshold value
AI generates a requirement; human approves; rule record updated; engine evaluates updated record
architecturalPrinciple: AI generates configuration, not code. The system executes configuration, not arbitrary AI-authored logic.

title: AI Requirements Target Named Configuration Fields — Not Free-Form Code
description: When AI maps a requirement to a plan or rule, the mapping targets named, typed configuration fields defined in the system schema. AI output can update eligibilityThresholdHours, waitingPeriodDays, employerContributionPercent, etc. AI output cannot generate or modify JavaScript/Node.js code.
prohibitedAITargets: arbitrary JSON keys not in schema

title: Premium Calculation Parameters Externalized in Rate Tables — Not Hardcoded
description: Premium amounts, employer contribution percentages, and pay-period divisors shall be read from seed/products/rates.json at runtime. The premium calculation formula (REQ-PREM-001) applies configurable values from the rate table; the formula itself is deterministic code.

title: Enrollment Window Durations Configurable per Life Event Type
description: Life event enrollment window durations (e.g., 30 days for Marriage, varies for other events) shall be read from the lifeEventRuleCatalog seed file. The application code reads the configuration; it does not contain hardcoded event-specific durations.

title: ACA Measurement Period Configurable in Employer Configuration
description: ACA measurement period start and end dates, and the ACA full-time threshold (30 hours), shall be stored in the employer configuration record. The ACA compliance engine reads these values; they are not hardcoded constants.

title: Notification Templates Configurable with Named Merge Fields
description: Employee notification message templates shall be stored as configurable records with named merge fields ({{employeeName}}, {{planName}}, {{effectiveDate}}). Template content changes shall not require code deployment.

title: Feature Flags Control Experimental Features via Environment Variables
description: Experimental or optional features shall be controlled by FEATURE_* environment variables defined in .env.example. Disabling a feature flag suppresses the UI element and disables the API endpoint — no code deletion required.

title: Carrier Simulation Mode Configurable (DETERMINISTIC vs CHAOS)
description: The carrier simulation behavior shall be controlled by the CARRIER_SIMULATION_MODE environment variable. DETERMINISTIC returns seeded scenario outcomes; CHAOS introduces randomized failures for monitoring stress-testing. The default for demo is DETERMINISTIC.

title: Dependent Age-Out Policy Configurable (Age, Cutoff Rule, Event Cascade)
description: The dependent age-out age (26), cutoff rule (end of birth month), and downstream cascade behavior (carrier TERMINATE, payroll stop) shall be configurable in the employer benefits configuration, not hardcoded. This supports future plan year policy updates without code changes.

title: Configuration Changes Create Audit Trail Entries
description: Any change to a configuration value (rate table update, rule threshold change, template text change, feature flag toggle) shall create an audit trail entry: who changed it, what the before/after values were, and when it was changed.

title: Eligibility Threshold Fields in Rule Engine Are Typed — Not Arbitrary Strings
description: Configurable eligibility fields shall have explicit types (INTEGER for hours threshold, DATE for effective dates, DECIMAL for contribution percentages, BOOLEAN for true/false conditions). The rule engine validates field types before evaluating. AI output targeting a typed field is validated against the field's type before being applied.

title: AI-Driven Configuration Change Requires Diff Preview Before Apply
description: When an approved AI requirement triggers a proposed configuration change ([Apply to Plan] or [Create Rule]), the system shall display a diff preview showing: current configuration values, proposed new values, and affected downstream entities. The user explicitly confirms before any change is persisted to a draft.
diffDisplay: Side-by-side: Current Value | Proposed Value | Change Source (REQ-ID, confidence, source document)
